**Anticapitalist Left** (*Sinistra Anticapitalista*, **SA**) is a small [far-left](/wiki/Far-left_politics "Far-left politics") [political party in Italy](/wiki/List_of_political_parties_in_Italy "List of political parties in Italy"), led by [Franco Turigliatto](/wiki/Franco_Turigliatto "Franco Turigliatto").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Anticapitalist_Left_(Italy)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e6/Sinistra_Anticapitalista_Primo_Maggio_2016_Torino_piazza_Castello.jpg/250px-Sinistra_Anticapitalista_Primo_Maggio_2016_Torino_piazza_Castello.jpg)](/wiki/File:Sinistra_Anticapitalista_Primo_Maggio_2016_Torino_piazza_Castello.jpg)

Members of Anticapitalist Left in 2016

Following the dissolution of [Critical Left](/wiki/Critical_Left "Critical Left") (SC) in 2013, Franco Turigliatto, Antonio Moscato, and other militants of the former SC, founded a new party, Anticapitalist Left.[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3) The new organization "promotes the elaboration and implementation of a [revolutionary communist](/wiki/Revolutionary_communist "Revolutionary communist"), [feminist](/wiki/Socialist_feminism "Socialist feminism"), [environmentalist](/wiki/Environmentalism "Environmentalism"), and [libertarian](/wiki/Libertarian_socialism "Libertarian socialism") political program".[[4]](#cite_note-4) In anticipation of the [Italian election of 2018](/wiki/2018_Italian_general_election "2018 Italian general election") and after having started a dialogue with the [Workers' Communist Party](/wiki/Workers%27_Communist_Party_(Italy) "Workers' Communist Party (Italy)"), SA decided to join [Power to the People](/wiki/Power_to_the_People_(Italy) "Power to the People (Italy)").[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6) On 4 October, the Anticapitalist Left decided to abandon the project, deciding that working with former OPG and Eurostop members to give life to a new party would have distorted the original project of a common, open, pluralist and inclusive front.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] At its second national congress, held in February 2019, the Anticapitalist Left became the Italian section of the [Fourth International](/wiki/Fourth_International "Fourth International").[[7]](#cite_note-7)
